source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane Man Rider

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Garden Banks Block 161

event.top_event: Hand entrapment in winch sheave during wireline valve assembly hoisting
actions_taken: Stop work request initiated by injured worker
CTLF operator lowered main winch to free trapped hand
Worker lowered to rig floor and escorted to Rig Medic's office
Helicopter evacuation to University of Texas Medical Branch at Galveston
Onsite BSEE investigation conducted on 15 March 2018
event.summary: On 14 March 2018 at 16:40 hours, a Helix Energy Solutions Roughneck sustained serious injuries to his left hand during well abandonment operations on the Helix Q4000 semisubmersible vessel. While hoisting a wireline valve assembly using a coiled tubing lift frame (CTLF) attached to a multipurpose tower, the worker observed the assembly beginning to shift and potentially contact the CTLF. In an attempt to reposition the assembly, the worker placed his left hand on the static line above the sheave on the CTLF main winch. His hand became trapped between the main winch sheave and the static line cable, resulting in partial amputation of the left thumb and severe laceration of the left ring finger. The worker was evacuated by helicopter and required surgery.

hazards.name: Rotating/moving machinery - winch sheave and cable
Pinch point between sheave and static line cable

threats.name: Improper hand placement on static line during hoisting
Inadequate stabilization of wireline valve assembly causing shift
Failure to recognize pinch point hazard in pre-job planning

consequences.name: Partial amputation of thumb and laceration of ring finger
Serious injury requiring helicopter evacuation and surgery

controls.name: Permit to Work
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure work is planned and authorized

supporting_text: A permit to work, four job safety analyses (JSAs), and a man riding checklist were prepared prior to work activities and reviewed by all personnel involved in this task.

controls.name: Job Safety Analyses (JSAs)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and safe work procedures

supporting_text: inadequate pre-job preparation and job hazardous analysis failed to recognize specific pinch points during CTLF main wench lifting operations

controls.name: Man Rider Checklist
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control for personnel working at height on lifting equipment

supporting_text: A permit to work, four job safety analyses (JSAs), and a man riding checklist were prepared prior to work activities and reviewed by all personnel involved in this task.

controls.name: Engineering controls for load stabilization
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent load shift during hoisting

supporting_text: the CTLF main wench lifting operational procedures had insufficient engineering controls in place to stabilize the wireline valve assembly from striking the CTLF
the design of the CTLF created clearance problems when lifting a wireline valve assembly

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt unsafe operations and prevent injury escalation

supporting_text: He initiated a stop work request and directed the CTLF operator to lower the main winch to free his left hand.
since CTLF lifting operations during this incident were routine, the crew was not accustomed to implementing stop work authority

controls.name: Operator supervision and oversight
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure safe and workmanlike operations

supporting_text: McMoRan failed to provide adequate supervision when a Helix Roughneck sustained injuries to his left hand from a sheave while hoisting a wireline safety valve using a coiled tubing lift frame

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
